visualizerParams.title: Binary to Octal Padding Bug
visualizerParams.problemDescription: The binary to octal conversion is failing for bitstrings whose length is not a multiple of 3. The bits are being grouped incorrectly from the left instead of the right. Find and fix the padding logic.

solution.explanation: Octal digits are formed by groups of 3 bits. If the bitstring length is not a multiple of 3, you must pad the LEFT side with zeros to ensure the bits are weighted correctly. Without padding, the remaining bits at the end would be processed as a partial triplet incorrectly.
